w:r.w:t: Ultimate 150-Day Java + DSA Mastery Roadmap (2026 Edition)
Master Java, Data Structures, Algorithms, Interview Patterns, and Problem Solving deeply enough for internships, coding rounds, competitive programming foundations, and advanced technical interviews.
Every day introduces a focused topic
Includes definitions, algorithm intuition, and practical implementation goals
PHASE 1 — Java Foundations & Core Problem Solving (Day 1–30)
Understanding how Java code compiles and runs using JVM.
Variables store data in memory using fixed-size primitive types.
Scanner reads input streams from keyboard.
Constant vs linear vs logarithmic growth
Using two indices to optimize traversal.
Finds maximum sum contiguous subarray in O(n).
Precompute cumulative sums for fast range queries.
Maintaining a window of fixed size over data.
Max sum subarray of size K
Mutable string container for efficient modifications.
Grids stored as arrays of arrays.
Functions solving smaller versions of themselves.
Break problem into smaller independent parts.
Finding insertion positions in sorted arrays.
Binary search on partially ordered arrays.
Find majority element in linear time.
Dynamic linear data structure using nodes.
Insertion and deletion in linked structures.
Optimized queue for sliding window problems.
Complete binary tree for prioritized retrieval.
Day 69 — Heaps vs BST vs HashMap
Shortest path for non-negative weighted graphs.
BFS starting from multiple nodes simultaneously.
Choose local optimum for global optimization.
Efficient pattern matching using LPS array.
Day 146 — Most Important Sliding Window Problems
Day 148 — Most Important Binary Search Problems
This is now a near-complete interview-grade DSA roadmap for 2026.
